summary_en: Second tender offer by ECM Master Fund SPV 3 closed 6 Mar 2026 (settlement 13 Mar). ECM voting stake post-offer fell below 2/3 threshold, making squeeze-out procedure uncertain. No squeeze-out agenda items included in 26 Jun 2026 AGM. ECM and company in ongoing discussions; no decisions made as of disclosure date.
title_en: Notice Regarding Future Outlook on Procedures for Going Private of Company Shares
translation_en_brief:
On 15 May 2026, SOFT99 Corporation disclosed an update on the status of going-private procedures initiated by ECM Master Fund SPV 3. ECM's second tender offer (second round) ran from 22 January to 6 March 2026 and closed successfully on settlement date 13 March 2026. Following the offer, however, ECM's voting stake in the company fell short of the 2/3 threshold needed to execute a squeeze-out merger without further shareholder approval.

As of the disclosure date, the board confirmed that no squeeze-out agenda items will be presented at the company's 72nd Ordinary General Meeting of Shareholders scheduled for 26 June 2026. ECM and the company are engaged in ongoing discussions regarding squeeze-out procedures, but no decisions have been reached. The disclosure states that the company will continue to deliberate with ECM and major shareholders on the implementation of squeeze-out procedures, taking into account corporate value enhancement and shareholder interests, and will announce material developments as they occur.
